item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my god, that 'Foundation Medical' would have been perfect for any of my bacteria idea, you got to it first. Anyways, great article, definite +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the foundation medical theme and the amount of effort put into this. It's a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: damn, how did i miss this fantastic post

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: TheMightyMcB, thank you for the specifics and I've addressed most of them. (I still can't quite get the "Search this site" text to be darker, but I plan to make it as you suggested.) Please let me know your thoughts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a great read, nice job man! The article is a treat in itself. +1, keep up the good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The search bar is looking golden now, I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I played with the search bar, I think it's cleaner now. Thank you for being specific about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To make my issues clear:
I dont like the search bar's colors (i understand the need for it to be a different color from the gradient) nor the Caduceus logo, which I feel is way too bright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for saying so; the color wheel agrees but to each their own.

A fun fact about it though is that it is modeled after the charting software I work with at my job, so that it appears like I’m still doing work when I’m actually done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really get the gripes some have with the theme, it''s easy on the eyes, and the shades are alluring.

As for the article, I've got to say it's one of the more pleasantly amusing reveals we've got here, and while all the pieces were in place, I wouldn't have pegged what was actually going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A well-written, intriguing article about a contagious fart.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gotta concur with others above. Not a fan of the theme, but the article is stellar.

EDIT: I'm not a fan of the following aspects of the theme:

  • The Foundation Medical logo is very bright and difficult to discern. You basically have to squint to actually see that it is supposed to have a caduceus in there. Much better.
  • The "Search this site" text in the search bar is hard to read, even though the search bar itself looks fine. Black text would look much cleaner.
  • The title of your article at the top is not a good choice of color in my opinion. A lighter grey would stand out much better.
  • The rating module is probably the worst part. White text on a light gray background is hell to read and unpleasant to look at. Again, black text would be much better.

The rest of the theme actually looks pretty nice.

EDIT: Based on what you've fixed, I'd say that the theme is pretty damn stellar right now. Super clean and immediately eye-catching.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Suggestions is welcome.

I don’t understand why this is, but the text is actually white in the code, on my mobile, and most of the time on my browsers. I can make it turn very dark when I resize the browser window to a specific range of width. (A similar jonkyness happens for the "Info Pages" link on the site's topbar at those browser widths; it gets pulled to the left of the browser and down a line.) Something of this effect happens with most CSS themes, it seems, even Sigma-9.

If you for some reason see the dark text instead, resize your browser window, mine turns white.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Though I'm a fan of your work, I strongly dislike the Medical theme. x_x

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The styling is cool, but "Foundation Medical" is really hard to read. I would make it a lighter color.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Having the pleasure to read this skip on the draft forums and then see it here brings me such joy. A wonderful addition to the mainlist and excellent break from the format while maintaining a scientific presentation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A Foundation-fronted television show developed and aired beginning in the 1990's, whereby civilians reported medical anomalies that were then investigated for possible classification, thereby crowd-sourcing and expediting discoveries.

I went into a gigglefit here. This was such a pleasant read when I saw it on your sandbox on IRC. +1. You make some of the highest quality content on the wiki in this wonderfully clinical tone that never meanders.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a Category 1 Cliche-Con entry. The featured cliche is:

  • "An anomaly so helpful/harmless, the Foundation lets it roam the Site and has minimal containment procedures. Being helpful/harmless is optional."
The reason for it in this article is because letting the anomaly do so is conducting research.

Thanks to all who helped me squeak this one out.
